articles.title: Suspected mortar shell brought into Essex police station prompts evacuation
articles.description:
A bomb squad later determined the device was a harmless, empty canister, authorities say.

The post Suspected mortar shell brought into Essex police station prompts evacuation appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Authorities evacuated Essex police station after a suspected mortar shell was brought in.
Bomb squad determined the device was a harmless, empty canister, ensuring public safety.
Local residents experienced temporary disruption due to the police station evacuation.

articles.title: Snag one-way flights for as low as $59 on Southwest
articles.description:
The sale ends March 19.

The post Snag one-way flights for as low as $59 on Southwest appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Southwest Airlines offers one-way flights from Boston starting at $59.
The promotional sale ends on March 19, 2026.
Boston residents can save significantly on travel costs this spring.

articles.title: City councilors call on Boston police to release footage of fatal shooting
articles.description:
Stephenson King was wanted in connection with an alleged carjacking when he was shot by police in Roxbury last week, officials said.

The post City councilors call on Boston police to release footage of fatal shooting appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: City councilors demand Boston police release footage of Stephenson King's fatal shooting.
King was wanted for an alleged carjacking prior to being shot in Roxbury last week.
Transparency in police actions may influence community trust and ongoing investigations.

articles.title: JPMorganChase inks deal to become the main tenant of new South Station Tower

summary: JPMorganChase becomes the main tenant of the new South Station Tower.

This expansion will create new jobs and boost local economy in Boston.

articles.title: ‘It feels like old times’: As Jayson Tatum’s minutes ramp up, so does chemistry with Jaylen Brown

Improved teamwork may lead to a stronger playoff run, benefiting Boston fans and the community.

articles.title: Mass. man gets 10 years for child porn possession after Google reports him
articles.description:
A tip from Google to a national watchdog group led to the investigation that found hundreds of images of child abuse materials on the man’s computer, authorities say.

The post Mass. man gets 10 years for child porn possession after Google reports him appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Massachusetts man sentenced to 10 years for possessing child pornography.
Hundreds of abusive images found on his computer after Google's report.
Case highlights the importance of tech companies in preventing child exploitation.

articles.title: In a region desperate for fresh retail, Bow Market is ‘a total unicorn situation.’ Why?
articles.description:
SOMERVILLE — In 2016, Zachary Baum and Matthew Boyes-Watson walked into Somerville City Hall with an unorthodox vision: to transform a vacant, U-shaped garage nestled behind a Union Square alleyway into a bustling bazaar of shoebox-sized storefronts called Bow Market. The pitch raised plenty of eyebrows among the regulators, financiers, and neighbors whose buy-in they […]

The post In a region desperate for fresh retail, Bow Market is ‘a total unicorn situation.’ Why? appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Bow Market opened in Somerville, transforming a vacant garage into 30 unique retail spaces.
Founded by Zachary Baum and Matthew Boyes-Watson in 2016, it revitalizes Union Square.
Local businesses report increased foot traffic and community engagement since Bow Market's launch.

articles.title: Local obituary: Matthew McTiernan, 22, Marshfield native and Johnson & Wales student
articles.description:
"Matt had a deep love for baking and the culinary arts. ... He especially loved baking bread, particularly his sourdough."

The post Local obituary: Matthew McTiernan, 22, Marshfield native and Johnson & Wales student appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Matthew McTiernan, 22, from Marshfield, passed away, impacting local community and culinary students.
He was a Johnson & Wales student with a passion for baking, especially sourdough bread.
His love for culinary arts inspired many in the Boston area, fostering local baking culture.
